snippets.title: A generator with yield
snippets.code:
def count_up(n):
    i = 0
    while i < n:
        yield i
        i += 1

gen = count_up(3)
print(next(gen))   # 0
print(list(gen))   # [1, 2]  -- continues from where it paused
snippets.explanation: yield pauses the function and resumes on the next request; calling the function returns a generator object.

snippets.title: The iterator protocol by hand
snippets.code:
it = iter([10, 20])
print(next(it))   # 10
print(next(it))   # 20
try:
    next(it)
except StopIteration:
    print("exhausted")   # for loops catch this automatically
snippets.explanation: iter() gives an iterator; next() advances it; StopIteration signals the end (handled by for loops).

snippets.title: Lazy streaming and yield from
snippets.explanation: Generators stream items one at a time; yield from re-yields everything from a sub-iterable.

snippets.title: Streaming LLM tokens as a generator
snippets.explanation: Token streaming is a generator of chunks, letting the UI display output as it is produced.
